hard.next_step: Inviting to EY event in OCtober WIll be inviting to our EY event in October This is a long play deal - we are keeping them warm until they are ready for RFP Conducted on-site discovery and demonstration meeting at the customer's offices on 15th May 2026 with Rachel and James. Rachel showed strong positive engagement with the Zycus platform and Merlin AI capabilities. James has confirmed attendance at the Zycus procurement event next week — good opportunity to deepen the relationship in a peer environment. Rachel is impressed with what Zycus can deliver but has raised a significant concern around internal resource availability to support a procurement transformation programme. She does not currently have the internal headcount or bandwidth to run an implementation project. This is the primary blocker to near-term progression. Rachel's intention is to issue an RFP towards end of 2026. Zycus is being positioned to help her build the business case and shape the RFP — giving us a strong influence position ahead of formal process launch.
hard.ais_why: • The SF Opportunity Description records Greencore's strategic context: 'The organization is currently undergoing a strategic transformation program called Making Business Easier (MBE), aimed at modernizing its supply chain, improving operational efficiency, and enabling data-driven decision-making across the enterprise.' Following the Bakkavor acquisition reaching ~£4B revenue, Greencore has 'significantly expanded its scale... increased operational complexity and accelerated the need for digital transformation.' Full Zycus AI suite in scope (ANA, Merlin, Merlin Intake) aligned to this MBE program.
hard.competitor: Pactum; Scanmarket; DocuSign CLM; Click (incumbent point tools, no suite rival engaged)

forecast_read.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (category is right; reset the close date to H1 2027)
forecast_read.reason: Pipeline is the honest category for an unbudgeted, pre-RFP deal, but the recorded close date of 2026-04-12 passed 88 days ago while Rachael Coad's own stated plan (10 Apr 2026 call) is to issue an RFP only toward the end of 2026. This is a date problem on an otherwise healthy early deal, not a deal problem.
forecast_read.math: 88 days past the 2026-04-12 close date as of 2026-07-09; 73 days in Qualified (Qualified_Submission_Date__c 2026-04-27); the close date was edited from 2027-04-12 to 2026-04-12 on 2026-05-12, the same day Amount moved £150k to £450k (OpportunityFieldHistory) — a data-entry artifact, not a pull-in. Buyer-stated RFP toward end of 2026 puts a realistic close in H1-H2 2027.

metrics.narrative: Rachael Coad quantified the scale of the pain herself on the 10 Apr 2026 discovery call: ~£650M annual indirect spend with roughly 30% in unmanaged tail across 2,000 live suppliers (80% of them tail), ~£2B direct spend, 7 ERP systems, and a publicly announced £80M synergy target from the Bakkavor acquisition that she said is 'not linked to any solutions at the moment' but 'an S2P system would really help'. What is missing is the translation into a Zycus business case: Anthony Gray committed on that call to send an example savings model and no delivery is confirmed, and Rachael's whole plan hinges on a business case she can take to leadership ('an absolute no brainer... press the button and go ahead').
sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-04-10 (Rachael Coad, verbatim transcript)

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named anywhere. Rachael refers only to 'the powers that be', 'the business' and 'my boss' (unnamed) as the audience for the business case, and said on 10 Apr that 'the business isn't quite as enthusiastic as me yet' and that budget sign-off comes after the business case. Supportive 'conditioning conversations' with leadership have happened (her words, 10 Apr) but no budget approver has been identified, met, or mapped. This is the single biggest qualification hole and it caps how far the deal can be believed.

decision_criteria.narrative: Formal criteria do not exist yet because the RFP is planned only toward end of 2026, but the shape is visible from the 10 Apr call: one integrated data core replacing disparate point tools (her recurring complaint), multi-ERP integration across System 21, SAP and Dynamics 365, AI-led automation (she asked specifically about the savings-opportunity agent and tail-spend consolidation), and demos that are 'interactive, live and not boring' with buyer-set use cases. Zycus is actively shaping the criteria: Anthony offered the RFP primer document and Zycus is positioned to help author the business case and RFP.
sources: SF next step note (deal owner, Jun 2026)

decision_process.narrative: Rachael described the process on 10 Apr in her own words: build the business case first, take it to leadership for 'the rubber stamp', then go to market with an RFP ('I want to build a business case, and then we're gonna go to market'), after which 'a whole team will be put together... project managers, IT, business analysts'. The deal owner's June note confirms the RFP is intended toward end of 2026. Timing is soft, no named approvers, and the process has no dated milestones yet.

paper_process.narrative: Nothing is known about contracting mechanics, and nothing should be at this stage: the deal is pre-budget and pre-RFP. The only relevant fact captured is that Greencore works with about 5 systems integrators today (Rachael, 10 Apr), which will matter later for delivery partnering and for answering her resourcing concern. No contract-expiration forcing function has surfaced on the incumbent point tools.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is rich, specific and owned by Rachael Coad, verbatim on 10 Apr: procurement has no PO visibility ('I've never seen a purchase order'), suppliers are 'not managed' with no due diligence, contracts auto-renewed because 'nobody has managed contract dates', spend visibility via Click 'works well for directs, not well for indirects', and a site was caught buying engineering day-rate resource from its nuts-and-bolts supplier. The organizational driver is the Making Business Easier transformation after the Bakkavor acquisition doubled the company to ~£4B. Confirmed, not inferred.

champion.narrative: Rachael Coad (Senior Controller - Indirect Procurement, and the SF-tagged Decision Maker) is a genuine champion: 'I will be running the project, and it looks like at the moment I will be owning the solution... I'm deeply invested... I'm like a big fan' (10 Apr), and she hosted the on-site demo at her offices on 15 May. Her limits are authority and bandwidth, not conviction: budget sits above her, and on 15 May she raised that she lacks the internal headcount to run an implementation programme, which the deal owner recorded as the primary blocker. Her advocacy has not yet been tested in front of leadership.
sources: SF next step note 15 May 2026 on-site

competition.narrative: No external S2P suite is engaged; the field today is Greencore's own disconnected point tools, all named by Rachael on 10 Apr: Scanmarket (eProcurement, 'doesn't tie into anything'), DocuSign CLM as a repository ('doesn't tie into anything'), Click for spend visibility (weak on indirects), plus two AI tools she rates highly: Pactum for autonomous negotiation ('we're in our second year of having it now') and an unnamed AI contract-redlining tool ('which I love. It is the best thing ever'). She also said plainly that she will work 'with you guys and with some of your competitors' on the business case, so a full shortlist forms at the end-2026 RFP. Zycus's edge is shaping that RFP first.

critical_signals.text: The recorded close date of 12 April 2026 has already passed while Rachael Coad's stated plan (10 Apr) is to issue an RFP only toward the end of 2026, so the realistic close sits in 2027.

The 15 May on-site demo at Greencore's offices landed well with Rachael Coad and James Dean, but Rachael raised that she lacks the internal headcount to run an implementation programme, naming it the main blocker to near-term progress.

No rival suite is engaged yet, but Rachael already runs Pactum for autonomous negotiation (second year) and loves a separate AI redlining tool, so Zycus must displace tools she considers working when her RFP forms.
Zycus has been invited to help build Greencore's business case and shape the RFP ahead of the formal process, a front-runner influence position no competitor currently holds.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: An early, genuinely well-qualified deal with a strong champion and buyer-quantified pain, deliberately parked by the buyer until an end-2026 RFP. Position is capped by the missing economic buyer, the unbudgeted status and the pre-RFP stage; momentum is low because there has been no buyer activity since the 1-2 Jun email declines and no future buyer session is booked. The close date is a data artifact that has already passed.

win_position.text: Rachael Coad is a confirmed, invested champion who will run and own the S2P project ('I'm deeply invested... I'm like a big fan', 10 Apr) and hosted the on-site demo at her offices on 15 May.
Pain and scale are buyer-quantified: ~£650M indirect spend with ~30% unmanaged tail, 7 ERPs, no PO visibility, and an £80M synergy target she says an S2P system 'would really help' (10 Apr).
No rival suite is engaged and Zycus is positioned to co-author the business case and shape the RFP before the field forms (deal owner note, Jun 2026).
No economic buyer is identified: budget sits with unnamed leadership ('the powers that be') and Rachael concedes 'the business isn't quite as enthusiastic as me yet' (10 Apr).
The deal is unbudgeted and pre-RFP by the buyer's own design, and Rachael's 15 May resourcing concern (no internal headcount to run an implementation) is the stated primary blocker.

deal_momentum.text: No buyer activity in over a month: the last buyer touches were Rachael's 1 Jun and James's 2 Jun email replies, both declining the 4 Jun EY event, and no meeting has happened since the 15 May on-site demo.
No dated future buyer session exists; the October EY event is an invitation plan, not a booked meeting, and the 16-17 Jun outreach emails to James Dean drew no reply.
The slowdown is explained and mutual: the deal owner is deliberately keeping Greencore warm until Rachael is ready for her end-2026 RFP, which keeps the deal alive rather than dark.
Through early June the engagement was genuinely two-way: both contacts replied personally, and James asked to 'include me on any future discussions/events' (2 Jun).

customer_commitment.text: Rachael drove the April scheduling herself, supplied her own demo use cases ('I've done some use cases, let me know if there are too many', 27 Apr), and gave Zycus a 3-hour on-site slot on 15 May.
Since 15 May the buyer's only actions are two polite event declines (1-2 Jun); commitment is real but parked behind her business-case and resourcing constraints.

deal_risk.text: The close date (12 Apr 2026) has already passed and was created by a field edit on 12 May (2027 pulled to 2026, same day the amount tripled to £450k), so the forecast date is not credible.
Rachael has no internal headcount to run a transformation programme (15 May), and until that is answered she cannot credibly sponsor the project to leadership.

The deal is effectively two-threaded (Rachael Coad and a quieter James Dean) with no access to the budget approver; a leadership change or priority shift inside the crowded MBE programme could stall it silently.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer-voiced enthusiasm is for the S2P opportunity, not yet for Zycus specifically: Rachael said 'I'm like a big fan' of the transformation and answered 'Yeah' when asked if the Zycus story resonated (10 Apr); the 'strong positive engagement with the Zycus platform' after the 15 May demo is the rep's read, not the buyer's words. She has stated she will also engage competitors for the business case.

business_case.evidence: Pain is quantified by the buyer (£650M indirect, ~30% tail, £80M synergy target, 10 Apr call) but no ROI model or example business case is confirmed delivered despite Anthony Gray's 10 Apr commitment to send one; the business case Rachael needs for leadership sign-off does not yet exist.

deal_movement.summary: Real movement stopped in mid-May: the 15 May on-site discovery and demo was the last buyer session, followed by two buyer email declines of the 4 Jun EY event. Everything since is rep-side or internal CRM motion (ownership and co-owner changes, MASE to-do echoes).

items.change: Shriya Iyer added as opportunity co-owner; Division field toggled (internal CRM housekeeping)
James Dean declined the 4 Jun EY event by email but asked to be included in future discussions and events
Rachael Coad declined the 4 Jun EY event by email
On-site discovery and demonstration meeting held at Greencore's offices with Rachael Coad and James Dean (held, not recorded)
Opportunity owner moved from Anthony Gray to Claire Hudson
Amount raised £150k to £450k and close date edited from 2027-04-12 to 2026-04-12 (the date edit is a data-entry artifact that left the close in the past)
Stage advanced Initial Interest to Qualified

day_summary.overall: The last day with real deal activity was 2 June, when James Dean replied to the EY event invitation: he declined the 4 June session (as Rachael Coad had the day before) but explicitly asked to be kept on future discussions and events. Since then the account has been in the deliberate keep-warm holding pattern the owner set for the end-2026 RFP, with only rep-side outreach and internal record changes.

items.name: James Dean reply on EY event invite
items.summary: Declined the 4 June Agentic AI in Procurement event but asked to be included in any future discussions and events

competitive_position.summary: No external S2P vendor is in a formal contest; the competitive picture is Greencore's own weak, disconnected point tools, all named by Rachael Coad on 10 Apr 2026. Scanmarket (eProcurement), DocuSign CLM (used as a contract repository) and Click (spend visibility, weak on indirects) are low-threat displacement targets she herself describes as not tying into anything. The real retention risks are Pactum, an embedded AI autonomous-negotiation tool in its second year that 'gets mentioned at every possible opportunity', and an unnamed AI contract-redlining tool she 'loves' — both defensible by the buyer as already working. The true shortlist forms at her end-2026 RFP ('working with you guys and with some of your competitors'); until then Zycus's advantage is authoring the business case and RFP primer. All competitive evidence is dated 2026-04-10; no calls since, so threat levels are carried forward.

competitors.name: Pactum
competitors.quote: Would we have an AI autonomous negotiation tool? I'd have laughed in your face, and we're in our second year of having it now... The AI autonomous negotiation tool, I swear to God, gets mentioned at every possible opportunity.
competitors.how_we_win: Demonstrate Merlin ANA negotiating inside one S2P data core versus a standalone point tool, so consolidating Pactum's job into the suite is a gain, not a loss.

competitors.name: Unnamed AI contract-redlining tool
competitors.quote: We've recently got a separate tool for contract red light AI contract redlining, which I love. It is the best thing ever.
competitors.how_we_win: Show iContract's AI redlining matching the loved point tool while adding the full CLM and repository she admits Greencore lacks.

competitors.name: Scanmarket
competitors.quote: We've got Scanmarket for eProcurement... a separate system that doesn't tie into anything.
competitors.how_we_win: Position eProcurement plus guided intake on one integrated data core, closing the indirect-spend control gap she flagged.

competitors.name: DocuSign CLM
competitors.quote: We're using Docusign as our repository. Docusign doesn't tie into anything.
competitors.how_we_win: Demonstrate iContract as a unified CLM on the same data core, removing the standalone repository and the auto-renewal leakage it has already caused.

competitors.name: Click
competitors.quote: From a spend visibility, we use click. Click works well for directs, not well for indirects.
competitors.how_we_win: Demonstrate unified spend analytics across direct and indirect spend where Click is admittedly weak.

competitors.name: Full S2P shortlist (forms at the end-2026 RFP)
competitors.quote: Working with you guys and with some of your competitors is being able to create a really compelling business case.
competitors.how_we_win: Own the RFP primer and business case now so Zycus sets the evaluation criteria before rivals engage; for this buyer, doing nothing means the tail stays unmanaged and the £80M synergy target goes unsupported.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Rachael's stated expectations map well to the Zycus pitch: one integrated data core, multi-ERP integration, AI-led automation and demanding live demos. The one exposed area is delivery resourcing: she has said she cannot staff an implementation, and Zycus has not yet answered that with a phased, partner-resourced plan.

items.criterion: One integrated platform replacing disparate point systems
items.quote: There's a real opportunity... get rid of all these disparate systems and have everything under one roof.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-04-10 (Rachael Coad)

items.criterion: Integration across 7 ERPs (System 21, SAP migration, Dynamics 365, Great Plains)
items.quote: We have multiple ERP systems... about 7.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-04-10; Anthony Gray referenced a client with 47 SAP instances connected

items.criterion: AI-led automation of tail spend and negotiation
items.quote: You buy paper clips from 17 different suppliers... consolidate all of that into this one supplier... and we'll negotiate a price for you. — 'Yes, and we'll demonstrate that in our workshop.'
items.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-04-10

items.quote: My ask is always that it's interactive, live, and, you know, just not boring.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-04-10; on-site demo delivered 15 May 2026

items.criterion: Implementation that does not depend on buyer headcount
items.quote: She does not currently have the internal headcount or bandwidth to run an implementation project.

items.requirement: Help build the business case Rachael can take to leadership for budget sign-off, and shape the RFP ahead of the formal process
items.quote: What I would like you to do is help me bring that to life to my business so I can get the rubber stamp, get the money, get going.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-04-10; re-confirmed in the deal owner's June 2026 next step note

items.requirement: Interactive, live, use-case-driven demo built on her supplied use cases

items.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-04-10; the 15 May demo was not recorded, so coverage of this ask is unconfirmed

items.deliverable: Example business case / normalized savings model for Greencore

items.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-04-10 (Anthony Gray); no confirmed delivery in any activity since

items.deliverable: RFP primer document (the world-leading S2P requirements primer)

items.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-04-10 (Anthony Gray); the 18 May 'Resources to Support Your Journey' email may have carried it but delivery is unconfirmed

items.deliverable: Spend and supplier data extracts from the 'green' and 'blue' data teams to feed the business-case build
items.grounding_quote: I have preempted what the data ask will be... I've got my request in to both green and blue teams.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-04-10 (Rachael Coad)

items.deliverable: Confirmation of Rachael and James attendance at the October EY event and a date for the next working session

items.quote: Working with... the powers that be within the organization who are also supportive.

items.area: Budget size and approval chain for the S2P line inside the MBE programme

items.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-04-10

best_practice_check.summary: A well-run discovery motion now at risk of drifting through its own keep-warm phase: the influence position (co-authoring the business case and RFP) is only real if Zycus delivers the promised material, answers the resourcing blocker, and reaches the budget approver before the end-2026 RFP freezes the criteria.
flags: Map and reach the economic buyer: budget sits with unnamed leadership and the business case has no confirmed audience; have Rachael name and broker the MBE budget approver as part of the business-case build.

Neutralize the resourcing blocker before the RFP: Rachael says she has no headcount to run an implementation (15 May); bring a phased, SI-partnered delivery model (Greencore already works with ~5 SIs) so the blocker never enters the RFP as a criterion Zycus fails.

Keep-warm needs substance, not events: both contacts declined the June event; the next touch should be the promised savings model and a dated August business-case working session, not another invitation.

items.sentiment: Highly invested in the S2P project and personally driving it; warm to Zycus but explicitly evaluating competitors too
items.risk: Sole real driver; SF tags her Decision Maker but budget authority sits above her, and she lacks implementation headcount (her stated blocker, 15 May)

champion_strength.summary: Rachael Coad is a confirmed, strong champion who expects to run and own the S2P solution ('I will be running the project... I'm deeply invested', 10 Apr) and hosted Zycus on-site on 15 May. Her constraints are authority (budget approval sits with unnamed leadership) and bandwidth (no internal headcount for an implementation, her stated primary blocker). Not at risk, but her advocacy is untested in front of the economic buyer.
champion_strength.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-04-10; SF next step note 15 May 2026; SF contact role

expansion_context.note: Net-new logo; no sibling Closed-Won opportunity on the account, so no inherited executive access.

scope_change.to: £450k full S2P suite including Merlin, Merlin Intake and ANA
scope_change.detail: Amount tripled from £150,000 to £450,000 on 2026-05-12 (OpportunityFieldHistory) after the 10 Apr discovery reframed a contract-repository need into a full source-to-pay transformation under the MBE programme; the product line now spans 11 modules.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus is positioned AI-first and the buyer is receptive: the agentic S2P story, the savings-opportunity agent and autonomous negotiation were the moments Rachael leaned into on 10 Apr, and the full AI product set (Merlin, Merlin Intake, ANA) is in scope. The bar is high because she already runs Pactum and an AI redlining tool she loves, so Zycus must prove superiority, not novelty.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Greencore is an AI-forward buyer: two years into Pactum autonomous negotiation, delighted with AI contract redlining, and Rachael says AI negotiation 'gets mentioned at every possible opportunity' internally. AIS 8.0 (Strong) corroborates.

items.detail: Close date 2026-04-12 has already passed; the buyer's own plan is an RFP toward end of 2026, so the recorded date is off by roughly a year and the deal will sit in a long keep-warm phase where drift is the default.
items.source: OpportunityFieldHistory 2026-05-12; Avoma call 2026-04-10; SF next step note Jun 2026

items.detail: Rachael has no internal headcount or bandwidth to run a procurement transformation programme; she named this the primary blocker to near-term progression (15 May on-site). Unanswered, it undermines both the business case and her sponsorship.

drivers: ✅ Confirmed, invested champion: Rachael Coad will run and own the S2P project ('I'm deeply invested... I'm like a big fan', 10 Apr) and hosted the on-site demo at her offices on 15 May.
✅ Buyer-quantified pain at scale: ~£650M indirect spend with ~30% unmanaged tail, 7 ERPs, no PO visibility, and an £80M synergy target she says an S2P system 'would really help' (10 Apr).
✅ No rival suite engaged: Zycus is co-authoring the business case and RFP primer before the end-2026 shortlist forms — the criteria-shaping seat.

⚠️ No economic buyer identified: budget sits with unnamed leadership and 'the business isn't quite as enthusiastic as me yet' (10 Apr). ► Have Rachael name and broker the MBE budget approver as the business-case audience.

⚠️ Unbudgeted, pre-RFP deal with a stated blocker: Rachael has no internal headcount to run an implementation (15 May). ► Bring a phased, SI-partnered delivery plan that removes the resourcing objection.

⚠️ Engagement cooling since 15 May: both contacts declined the 4 Jun event and no future session is booked. ► Deliver the promised savings model this week and book an August business-case working session.

⚠️ No buyer action in 37+ days: the last buyer touches were Rachael's 1 Jun and James's 2 Jun email replies (both event declines); nothing buyer-side since. ► Re-engage with the promised business-case material, not another invitation.

⚠️ No dated future buyer session: the October EY event is a plan, not a booked meeting, and the 16-17 Jun outreach to James drew no reply. ► Book an August business-case working session with Rachael and James.
⚠️ The keep-warm phase is consuming the calendar: last real meeting was the 15 May on-site demo, 55 days ago. ► Put one dated buyer milestone between now and the end-2026 RFP.
✅ The slowdown is explained and mutual: the owner is deliberately keeping Greencore warm until Rachael is ready for her end-2026 RFP, so this is a parked deal, not a dark one.
✅ Engagement was genuinely two-way through early June: both contacts replied personally and James asked to be included in future discussions (2 Jun).
⚠️ The only recent CRM motion is internal (co-owner added 7 Jul) and the close date has already passed. ► Reset the close date to the buyer's stated timeline so the plan back-solves from reality.

prospect_requirements: Deliver the business-case support Rachael Coad asked for on 10 Apr ('help me bring that to life to my business so I can get the rubber stamp') — co-build the leadership deck; target a working session by 2026-08-07.
